market_context: The Dec 4–16 window was shaped by three overlapping forces. First, the FOMC delivered an expected 25bp cut on Dec 10 (fed funds 3.50–3.75%) but the accompanying dot plot was starkly hawkish — just one cut projected for 2026, voted 9-3 with three dissenters, triggering a 'hawkish cut' re-pricing. Second, Broadcom (AVGO) reported blowout Q4 FY25 earnings on Dec 11 (beat on revenue/EPS, AI rev +74% YoY, Q1 guide $19.1B) yet collapsed 11% intraday then a further 5.6% Monday Dec 15, as margin pressure commentary from the earnings call reignited 'AI capex ROI' skepticism that spread to AMD, ARM, Oracle, Nvidia. Third, the November CPI was delayed (government shutdown data issues) and ultimately released Dec 18 — not Dec 10 as scheduled — so the Dec 4–16 window lacked the inflation catalyst, making the Fed's hawkish dot plot the dominant macro event. The net result: S&P 500 -0.6% week of Dec 12, Nasdaq -1.6%, semis -5%+ Friday Dec 12. Tech AI names faced the most acute selling pressure. Safe havens (GLD) held well. Rate-sensitive growth (ARM, CRM, COIN) underperformed structurally. Regime = hawkish-Fed + AI angst rotation, biased toward SHORT growth/tech and LONG defensives/gold.

trades.thesis: AVGO at near-ATH $379 with Q4 earnings scheduled Dec 11. Stock priced for perfection at ~35x forward PE on AI semiconductor thesis. Any commentary about margin pressure from custom ASIC ramp (Google TPU, Meta MTIA) would crush sentiment. Risk/reward skewed: upside limited by valuation; downside 15-20% on guidance concern.
trades.catalyst: Broadcom Q4 FY25 earnings Dec 11 — beat revenue/EPS but management flagged AI chip margin compression; 'AI angst' selloff -11% day-of, -5.6% following Monday. Total drawdown ~21% from pre-earnings ATH.
trades.lesson: Earnings put thesis on high-multiple AI hardware names at ATH has excellent asymmetry: a beat can still disappoint on margin outlook. Entering 1 week pre-earnings (not day-of) captured the full move including post-earnings drift.

trades.thesis: Salesforce reported Q3 FY26 on Dec 3 after close: EPS $3.25 vs $2.85 est (+14% beat), revenue $10.26B vs $9.98B est, raised FY26 guidance, Agentforce ARR hitting $1.4B at +114% YoY. Stock +4% Dec 4. The Agentforce product cycle gives CRM a credible AI monetization story distinct from infrastructure capex fear. Enterprise software with recurring revenue more defensible than semiconductor names.
trades.catalyst: Q3 FY26 earnings beat + raised guidance Dec 3; stock gap confirmed strong buyer demand Dec 4; Agentforce narrative intact.
trades.lesson: Post-earnings momentum plays on software with clean beats work even in hawkish rate environments if the AI monetization narrative is specific and verifiable (ARR metric). The trade was more modest (+3.7%) because broader market headwinds capped the move.

trades.thesis: AAPL at $278 (~31x forward PE) with no incremental AI catalyst — Apple Intelligence rollout behind schedule, China market share losses to Huawei accelerating, services growth decelerating. Rate sensitivity high at this multiple. No earnings catalyst to sustain premium vs peers. Risk: FOMC week uncertainty + hawkish dot plot would compress multiple on non-growth tech.
trades.catalyst: Pre-FOMC positioning; hawkish dot plot Dec 10 triggers tech multiple compression; AAPL has no near-term product catalyst to absorb rate headwind. Also: consumer-facing tech underperforms in hawkish regimes vs enterprise.
trades.lesson: Large-cap consumer tech without near-term catalyst is clean short in hawkish regimes. AAPL's premium valuation relative to growth profile makes it structurally exposed when rates signal higher-for-longer.

trades.thesis: ARM at $141.93 — most egregiously valued name in semis at ~100x forward earnings. Royalty model means revenue growth linear with TAM, not exponential. No proprietary AI differentiation: NVDA CUDA, AMD ROCm, and custom ASICs (Apple/Google/Amazon) route around ARM's software stack. FOMC week with hawkish lean = duration compression kills 100x multiples hardest. No near-term earnings catalyst to justify premium.
trades.catalyst: FOMC hawkish cut Dec 10; AVGO earnings disappointment Dec 11 triggers broad semis selloff; ARM trades in sympathy as highest-multiple semi; AI capex ROI skepticism hits royalty models hardest.
trades.lesson: The highest-multiple names in any sector provide maximum convexity to the short side during hawkish rate regime shifts. ARM at 100x is the clearest expression of duration risk in semis — when AI capex narrative wobbles, the correction is violent and non-linear.

trades.thesis: Gold entering FOMC week with dual tailwinds: (1) hawkish cut scenario = uncertainty about future cuts = flight to safety; (2) USD likely to strengthen short-term but gold had been building a base around $387 with strong physical demand from central banks. Gold tends to hold well in hawkish Fed surprises because the 'higher-for-longer = recession risk' narrative offsets USD strength.
trades.catalyst: FOMC Dec 10 hawkish cut signals slowdown in rate cuts; recession risk pricing boosts gold; GLD base at $387 acted as support; precious metals maintained bid through year-end.
trades.lesson: GLD is a clean long in hawkish-surprise FOMC environments when the hawkishness signals economic slowdown risk rather than pure inflation fighting. The 'higher-for-longer = growth uncertainty' narrative drives institutional gold demand irrespective of USD.

trades.thesis: Coinbase at $275 after Bitcoin had rallied hard post-election (Trump crypto-friendly). The FOMC hawkish dot plot (only 1 cut in 2026) is structurally negative for risk assets, and crypto is the highest-beta risk asset. COIN trades at ~8x revenue with revenues 100% correlated to crypto trading volumes. Hawkish Fed = lower retail trading activity + institutional risk-off = double headwind.
trades.catalyst: FOMC hawkish cut Dec 10; dot plot signals fewer future cuts than priced; BTC/ETH begin mean-reverting post-election euphoria; risk-off rotation crushes high-beta names; COIN -12.5% over 20TD.
trades.lesson: Crypto exchanges (COIN, MSTR) are maximum-beta instruments on Fed policy. A hawkish surprise that reduces rate-cut expectations crushes these names harder than any other sector because they depend on (1) risk appetite and (2) leveraged retail trading activity that dries up in tightening regimes.

trades.thesis: AMD at $221 post-AVGO earnings debacle. AMD's MI300X GPU was supposed to be the 'NVIDIA alternative' but hyperscaler procurement data showed minimal CUDA-equivalent software ecosystem. AVGO's earnings commentary about AI margin compression would pressure AMD's data center GPU narrative directly. AMD trading at ~40x forward PE with data center GPU ramp disappointing vs expectations set at AMD Financial Analyst Day.
trades.catalyst: AVGO earnings collapse Dec 11 triggers AI semiconductor risk-off; AMD specific: MI300X ramp slower than expected; no software moat equivalent to NVIDIA CUDA; hawkish Fed reduces multiple expansion capacity.
trades.lesson: Sympathy shorts in sectors hit by a major name's disappointing earnings (AVGO to AMD) work when the fundamentals are actually linked — AMD's AI GPU story shares the same AI capex uncertainty narrative. Entering day-of the primary catalyst (AVGO earnings day) captures maximum momentum.

trades.thesis: NVDA sold off ~5% week of Dec 12 in sympathy with AVGO, but the narratives are fundamentally different. NVDA's H100/H200/Blackwell ecosystem has actual software lock-in (CUDA, cuDNN, NIM microservices). AVGO's weakness is about ASIC margins — which actually validates NVDA's GPU moat over custom silicon. At $175, NVDA trading at ~25x CY26 earnings, a level last seen pre-AI acceleration. Strong support at $170-175.
trades.catalyst: Post-AVGO sympathy oversell creates entry; NVDA Blackwell demand backlog still 6+ months; differentiated from AVGO's margin issues; support at $175 = 50-day MA level.
trades.lesson: When a sector selloff is driven by a tangential name (AVGO custom ASICs), the market leader with genuinely differentiated fundamentals (NVDA CUDA ecosystem) presents a mean-reversion long opportunity. Key distinction: AVGO's issue is margin compression on ASIC commoditization, which actually strengthens NVDA's GPU moat narrative.

trades.thesis: Second GLD entry as the risk-off rotation continued into the week of Dec 15. AI capex skepticism, hawkish Fed, and year-end tax-loss harvesting in tech create systematic inflows to safe havens. Gold had broken above $395 resistance with conviction. Central bank demand (China, Poland, India) provided a structural bid independent of rate expectations. Year-end position squaring further supported gold as hedge funds reduced equity risk.
trades.catalyst: Continued AI tech selloff week of Dec 15; year-end risk reduction; gold technical breakout above $395 resistance; institutional rebalancing into safe havens pre-year-end.
trades.lesson: GLD is one of the cleanest year-end longs in hawkish-rate environments: (1) year-end portfolio hedging demand is secular, (2) central bank buying provides a structural floor, (3) tax-loss harvesting in equities redirects capital to non-correlated assets. Double-dipping on GLD confirmed the structural regime thesis.

trades.thesis: Shorting CRM which had risen to $253 after the Dec 4 post-earnings bounce. The initial enthusiasm for Agentforce faces a hard reality check: $1.4B ARR on a $41.5B revenue base means Agentforce is <4% of revenue. SaaS multiples compress structurally in higher-for-longer environments (25x revenue unjustified at 9% growth). CRM's operating cash flow guide +13-14% implies limited upside to consensus. The Dec 4 long trade captured the earnings bounce — now fading the move.
trades.catalyst: Post-earnings euphoria fade; SaaS multiple compression in hawkish Fed regime; Agentforce ARR too small to move the needle; AI SaaS growth deceleration narrative (Salesforce, ServiceNow) gaining traction; triple witching week volatility accelerates moves.
trades.lesson: Fading post-earnings momentum in SaaS names where the AI growth story is overhyped relative to actual ARR contribution is a repeatable pattern. CRM's Agentforce at <4% of revenue doesn't justify the premium priced in post-earnings. Short the fade after initial enthusiasm wears off.

period_summary.regime_notes: The Dec 4–16 regime was a classic hawkish-cut inflection: market initially priced in a dovish Fed pivot, then the Dec 10 dot plot (only 1 cut in 2026, 9-3 vote) triggered a regime reclassification. The directional signal was clear: SHORT high-multiple growth tech (ARM, AAPL, AMD, COIN, CRM SaaS fade), LONG safe havens (GLD). AVGO earnings were the sector-specific landmine — a 'beat and drop' that validated the AI capex margin-compression thesis. The short book carried the portfolio: 6 shorts averaged +10.14%, 4 longs averaged +5.93%. Key regime markers that confirmed the trade direction: (1) three FOMC dissenters = unusual hawkish conviction, (2) dot plot cut 2026 forecast from 4 to 1 = massive guidance shift, (3) AVGO earnings call management commentary explicitly mentioning ASIC margin compression = AI infrastructure thesis under pressure. No losses recorded because the macro regime shift was identified pre-event and positions were sized with correct directional bias.
